intro: Siphocampylus sonchifolius, also known as sonchifolius or golden groundsel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Siphocampylus sonchifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the production of herbal teas and tinctures.
